item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Initially i was going to give this a down vote because of how unreasonably vague it felt. But that ending definitely made up for it in my opinion, that was a great little twist on an otherwise mildly weird bird.

For me personally there is a conflicting feeling, i think it might need a bit more development at the beginning lest it lose a reader too early, though it is short enough that may not be an issue. You eat the egg and act strangely and then there is an "egg shaped mass" at the base of your spine, but then of course too much development would ruin the rather fun ending of the interview.
Just my own personal thoughts on it, i can't say i have a solution to suggest, after finishing the article i very much enjoyed it so +1 for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Vernon Dorsey

Legit read that as Vernon Dursley first time round, was very confused. Great article, I think the escalation of danger was executed well. Also love the unusual ConProcs. +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
confession: i thought i was saving this on my sandbox but y'know what, sink or swim bby

Thanks to:

DrAkimoto
Cyvstvi13
Mew-ltiverse

The interview log format was inspired by The Magnus Archives

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC-BY-SA 4.0 International
Title: bird.jpg
Author: Zeynel Cebeci
Release year: 2016

[+] Quick notes for translators
– hide block
  • xe/xem pronouns aren't easy to translate: I hereby give permission to change them to (if available) they/them in translations or (if a neutral option isn't possible), he/him
  • There's a zero width space between [ and // in the action descriptions in the interview log, to prevent wikidot treating them as a link
